summary: Verified the finance-grade strict classification overhaul end-to-end. Backend run_matching() applies Rule A (pure ref + fully consumed => FULL) and Rule B (single debtor >=95 + exact amount + non-ambiguous => FULL); everything else with matches => PARTIAL, no signal => UNMATCHED. Per-row 'reason' field populated, per-match score/ambiguous/confidence populated. Frontend AllocationDetail tabs renamed to Confirmed/Suggested, new 'Why matched?' column visible with reason text, AlertTriangle icon appears next to ambiguous/low-conf partial rows, match line shows score% (e.g. 'debtor_name 90%'). Dashboard stat cards say 'Confirmed matches' and 'Suggested (review)'. The infamous 'A AND J WAST LTD FMQ' bank row is now correctly UNMATCHED with reason 'No invoice reference detected and no debtor-name match above threshold'.

minor.issue: fuzz.WRatio() called without processor=utils.default_process — in rapidfuzz 3.14 default processor is None, so matching is CASE-SENSITIVE. Real-world bank statements often arrive in ALL-CAPS (e.g. 'GLOBEX CORP') while invoice debtor names are mixed-case ('Globex Corporation'), scoring ~28% instead of ~90% and being dropped below the 70-threshold. This silently under-classifies many legitimate fuzzy matches as UNMATCHED. RECOMMEND: pass processor=utils.default_process or .lower() both sides before calling WRatio. Verified: WRatio('GLOBEX','Globex')=16.7, WRatio('GLOBEX CORP','Globex Corporation')=28.4, WRatio('globex corp','globex corporation')=90.0.

action_items: FIX: In /app/backend/server.py run_matching() Pass 2 (line 418), change `fuzz.WRatio(compare_text_clean, debtor_clean)` to `fuzz.WRatio(compare_text_clean, debtor_clean, processor=utils.default_process)` (add `from rapidfuzz import utils`). Without this, any ALL-CAPS bank text vs mixed-case debtor name fails to score, so Pass 2 fuzzy matching is effectively dead for typical UK bank-statement data.
critical_code_review_comments: Strict classification block (server.py 446-507) is clear and well-commented. Rule A and Rule B preconditions are conservative and finance-grade.

Rule B currently requires `len(ms) == 1` AND `not ref_methods` — so a bank row that matched by reference and ALSO debtor-name can never become FULL via Rule B. This is the intended strict behaviour but worth highlighting: such mixed-method rows always demote to PARTIAL even when fully consumed, which may surprise users when a reference+debtor double-confirm hit gets labelled Suggested. Consider adding a Rule C for ref+debtor combo with full consumption.
rapidfuzz processor bug noted above is the only real backend defect. All Pydantic models, status codes, async handlers and MongoDB _id exclusion look correct.

Frontend AllocationDetail.jsx and Dashboard.jsx terminology updates are clean and consistent. data-testid attributes preserved (stat-full, stat-partial, bank-table). AlertTriangle conditional rendering is correct: (hasAmbiguous || lowConfPartial).

Match line score display (`{m.method}{m.score ? ` ${m.score}%` : ''}`) treats score 0 as falsy and would hide it — not a real issue because score is only set on debtor matches and is never 0 in practice (threshold >=70).

success_rate.backend: 100% (7/7 strict classification tests + 7/7 prior bearer-auth tests = 14/14)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login + dashboard stat renames + AllocationDetail tabs Confirmed/Suggested + Why-matched column + AlertTriangle on ambiguous row + score% in match line — all verified visually with screenshots)

seed_data_creation: 7 transient allocation runs created with TEST_ prefix during pytest; one UI run TEST_ui_strict created during browser test. None cleaned up — no leakage risk, all isolated to admin user.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Strict classification verified 2026-05-20. Backend regression: pytest backend/tests/test_strict_classification.py — 7/7 PASS. Scenarios covered: (1) pure reference + exact amount => FULL/high with reason 'Invoice reference X matched and exact amount consumed'; (2) debtor fuzzy ~90% unique => PARTIAL/medium with reason 'Debtor name similarity 90.0%'; (3) debtor fuzzy 90% with multiple candidates => PARTIAL/medium with ambiguous=true and reason contains 'multiple candidates — needs review'; (4) no signal => UNMATCHED with reason 'No invoice reference detected and no debtor-name match above threshold' (verified for 'A AND J WAST LTD FMQ'); (5) ref but amount overflow => PARTIAL with 'unallocated' in reason; (6) reason str always present on every bank row; (7) stats counts consistent. KNOWN BUG to fix: fuzz.WRatio is case-sensitive without processor=utils.default_process — see action_items. After fix, re-run test_strict_classification.py — all 7 should still pass because they already use same-case inputs.

rapidfuzz_case_sensitivity.problem: Pass 2 fuzzy debtor matching uses fuzz.WRatio without a processor, but rapidfuzz 3.14 defaults to no processor (unlike older versions / thefuzz). Result: case differences between bank text and debtor name destroy the score.
rapidfuzz_case_sensitivity.reproduction: python -c "from rapidfuzz import fuzz; print(fuzz.WRatio('GLOBEX CORP','Globex Corporation'))" => 28.4 (would-be 90.0 with same case).
rapidfuzz_case_sensitivity.mitigation: from rapidfuzz import utils; fuzz.WRatio(a, b, processor=utils.default_process) — OR pre-lowercase both inputs in strip_corp_suffix output. Either is a one-line fix in server.py.
